steps.title: Find the axis (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly sketch a single vertical line down the middle of where the pot will sit. This is the pot's symmetry axis — every curve you add will balance around it.

steps.title: Block the pot body (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a tapered trapezoid around the axis — wider at the top than the bottom. Keep both sides roughly equal distances from the center line.

steps.title: Add the rim ellipse (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch a shallow ellipse across the top of the trapezoid. The pot opening is round, but seen from above it reads as an oval. A second flatter ellipse for the base helps the pot sit on the tiles.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the silhouette
steps.description: Switch to ink mode. Trace the two tapered sides and the front of the rim in one confident pass. Stop your back-rim line where it disappears behind the front edge — don't draw all the way through.

steps.title: Ink the rim band (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, add a second curve just below the top rim ellipse, parallel to it. That short band gives the pot a thickened lip — the most recognisable terracotta detail.

steps.title: Suggest the planting (ink)
steps.description: Add three or four loose stalks rising from the rim, each ending in a small leafy mark. Don't draw every leaf — a handful of stems is enough to say 'something growing in here.'

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and remove any pencil guide lines that distract from the ink. The faint axis line can stay if it reads as nice construction texture.
